💡 The future belongs to people who know how to leverage AI.

Which AI tool do you use every single day?

👇 Drop it in the comments.

Follow @Jeffar_AI for daily AI tools, prompts, and productivity hacks. 🔥

Most people are using one AI tool.

Top builders are running a full AI stack.

I see it constantly.

Someone spends 6 months getting good at ChatGPT.

Then wonders why they're still slower than the person next to them.

It's not about knowing one tool well.

It's about knowing which tool to reach for — and when.

A year ago, I had a handful of AI tools I trusted.

Today, the landscape looks completely different.

The people pulling ahead aren't using more tools.

They're using the right tools for the right jobs.

Here's how the best builders are splitting it in 2026:

→ Brainstorming & writing:
ChatGPT 5.5

→ Data analysis:
Rows (spreadsheet-native)
ChatGPT 5.5 (flexible workflows)

→ Data visualization:
Julius

→ Coding:
OpenAI Codex
Replit

→ Image generation:
Ideogram (text in images)
GPT Image 2 (high-quality visuals)

→ Video creation:
Sora 2 Pro
Google Veo 3.1

→ Short-form video:
Ossa
Syllaby

→ LinkedIn growth:
ChatGPT 5.5
Taplio

→ Video editing:
Adobe Premiere Pro
VEED

→ Graphic design:
Leonardo AI
Canva

→ AI agents:
ChatGPT Agent
n8n

→ Deep research:
Perplexity
ChatGPT Deep Research

→ Complex problem-solving:
Gemini Pro
ChatGPT 5.5 Thinking

The founders getting the most out of AI right now aren't chasing every new release.
They have a core stack.
They know it inside out.

And they only add new tools when there's a real gap to fill.

That's the real edge.
If you're building with AI, save this and the following @lihazadn_Ai 

And if you know someone still trying to do everything with one tool...
Send this their way.

What's the one AI tool you'd never drop from your stack?

Inserting the low-angle rig shot file taken with 28mm as an insert over the Changdeokgung 28mm footage, and finally compositing the virtual character on top.

I pulled the color-graded version from the LOG file's color space. Up to today it's still doable work so I can just queue up batch processing, but the action scene I scheduled for the day before next week's business trip is stressing me out. Will it even recognize everything properly...

I still have to crunch through the camera tracking and that rolling severed head footage I shot earlier too...

I already put in all the visual scenes from the severed head's POV as it rolls on the ground, but (I literally rolled the camera. I made a wire structure based on one that wouldn't crush an egg, built it myself, mounted the camera inside, and rolled it across the ground.)

If I have to insert every single angle and size visible during that exact 0.9-second movement as images and paint out the reflections of the structure...

I feel like I'm gonna lose my mind before I even finish. lol
Just looking at the footage for a second now is making me dizzy...

It's the stuff I rolled while dying of heat at Gongju Sanseong.

Sigh... Will my local AI even survive this...?
Should I try to get it done before the business trip...? lololol

Think about it. Maybe when I get there I'll see?? If there's a GPU sale going on in the US, wouldn't it be smarter to just buy one and bring it back...? lol

What the hell am I saying. I've already bought the damn thing. Fuck.
